item.title: Trump and Iran reach agreement that includes opening Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump and Iran reach agreement that includes opening Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Taveuni born Harold Nair to appear on fijivillage Straight Talk after undergoing one of the most advanced brain surgeries ever performed in WA
item.description: Taveuni born Harold Nair to appear on fijivillage Straight Talk after undergoing one of the most advanced brain surgeries ever performed in WA

item.title: Heavy Rain Warning in force for Fiji
item.description: A Heavy Rain Warning is now in force for Fiji as an active trough of low pressure gradually moves eastwards towards the country. The Nadi Weather Office says cloud and rain associated with the system are already affecting parts of Fiji and are expected to continue impacting the group until later tomorrow.
item.content:encoded:
A Heavy Rain Warning is now in force for Fiji as an active trough of low pressure gradually moves eastwards towards the country.

The Nadi Weather Office says cloud and rain associated with the system are already affecting parts of Fiji and are expected to continue impacting the group until later tomorrow.

They say occasional rain will become frequent and heavy from later today, accompanied by thunderstorms.

The Weather Office says conditions are expected to gradually improve in the western parts of the country by around midday tomorrow.

They say heavy rainfall may lead to flash flooding in flood-prone and low-lying areas, including informal settlements, businesses and Irish crossings, which could result in traffic disruptions.

There is also a risk of surface flooding on streets in urban areas with poor drainage systems.

item.title: Dream becoming reality for Nabavatu villagers

item.description: Palliative care is an essential component of Fiji's response to cervical cancer and should not be viewed as an optional add-on.The

item.title: 3-year-old Asinate fights on as family appeals for more blood donors
item.description: The family of three-year-old Asinate Waicalanavanua Coriakula is grateful for the support and response from the general public on donating towards her blood drive so far. Coriakula's mother, Rauca Vani, told fijivillage News that her baby is slowly showing signs of progress and is recovering well.
item.content:encoded:
The family of three-year-old Asinate Waicalanavanua Coriakula is grateful for the support and response from the general public on donating towards her blood drive so far.

Coriakula's mother, Rauca Vani, told fijivillage News that her baby is slowly showing signs of progress and is recovering well.

She is appealing to people with A positive blood type to donate blood, as her daughter remains in the Burns Unit at CWM Hospital in Suva.

Vani is urgently requesting that blood A positive donors can continue to donate this week to ensure her full recovery and give her a new lease of life.

Her doctors and loved ones are calling on members of the public with the same blood type to come forward and help give her a chance of recovery.

Anyone willing to donate can contact her mother on 2742942.

item.title: 26 lives lost in road accidents this year
item.description: It is tragic that 26 lives have been lost on our roads this year, compared to 29 deaths recorded during the same period last year. The Fiji Police Force says there are several habits drivers and members of the public can practise every day to help ensure the safety of all road users.
item.content:encoded:
It is tragic that 26 lives have been lost on our roads this year, compared to 29 deaths recorded during the same period last year.

The Fiji Police Force says there are several habits drivers and members of the public can practise every day to help ensure the safety of all road users.

Police stress that despite ongoing road safety reminders, lives continue to be lost due to irresponsible and inconsiderate driving.

They are urging drivers to inspect their vehicles before driving.

Drivers are also being reminded to get enough rest before travelling and to remain focused while behind the wheel.
